step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with the selection of odontoblasts (Option B). Odontoblasts are indeed the principal secretory cells located on the surface of the pulp, extending along the dentinal tubules where they actively participate in mineralization and respond to external stimuli by modulating ion fluxes across the dentino-enamel junction. Their unique position allows them to regulate microenvironmental conditions critical for overall tooth health. Disagreeing would imply focusing instead on fibroblast activity which does not involve direct interaction with dentinal tubules nor specific secretory functions related to pulp maintenance. 

**Answer:** B: Odontoblasts. Based on my agreement and elaboration above,
